meaning: vt.实践,履行,实行,结束,满足,完成,达到
examples: Without them you will not be able to fulfil the tasks you have before you.
The editors can give no guarantee that they will fulfil their obligations.
He failed to fulfil his early promise.
It is only by participating in this divine life that mortal, fragile human beings fulfil their potential.
First, fulfil existing commitments to spend 0.7 per cent of national income on aid.
